title: Thutmose Bust of Nefertiti
topic_path: New Kingdom and Third Intermediate Period

assessment_items.question_content:
**What leads scholars to believe Thutmose used this bust of Nefertiti as a model for other representations of Nefertiti? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/52f7882d843a81c5771e213576b8bfc7.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Found in the artist's studio, this sculpture lacks the degree of finish and precious materials that were common to Egyptian royal sculpture. Clues supporting the idea that this bust was a model do not include the style or inscription.

choices.content: The location where it was found
The inscription on the base
The idealized style of the figure
The bust's lack of finish

assessment_items.question_content:
**What allowed the artist to achieve the subtle modeling of the figure's cheeks and jawline?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A layer of plaster applied over the limestone core allowed sculptor to more easily achieve subtle modeling and the bust's elegant forms.

choices.content: A layer of plaster over the limestone
A fine chisel
The application of colored pigment
A layer of wax over the limestone

hints: Nefertiti was the powerful queen of the Egyptian king Akhenaten.

choices.content: Akhenaten.
Thutmose.
Hu-nefer.
Menkaure.
Osiris.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What about Thutmose's bust of Nefertiti is characteristic of art during the Amarna period of Egyptian history?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Typical of the Amarna period, the bust of Nefertiti differs dramatically from earlier traditions in Egyptian art.

choices.content: Its style is distinctly different from other periods of art.
It is heavily painted to show details of the figure's features.
It can be attributed to a specific artist of the period.
It idealizes the queen's beauty.
It depicts the queen as a divine figure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement does NOT apply to BOTH sculptures? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68075aa0f7e25a2c00feb74170a24636.jpg)
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5d5604788c0fc8e9cc63436d96fb55d7.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Typical of the distinctive style of the Amarna period, these sculptures represent the wife and mother of Akhenaten.
Likely a model for other images the queen, the bust of Nefertiti lacks the precious materials typically used in royal Egyptian sculpture.

choices.content: Both employ precious materials to enhance the beauty of the figures.
Both are idealized images of important women during Akhenaten's reign.
Both reveal subtle lines and contours of the women's facial features.
Both differ from established conventions of Egyptian art.
